pattern: Circular maximum-sum of n/3 non-adjacent elements using two linear DP runs that exclude the first or last element.
hints: The constraint that your neighbours always take adjacent slices means you can never pick two consecutive slices; this reduces to: pick exactly n/3 non-adjacent elements from a circular array for maximum sum.
Circular non-adjacency is handled by running two standard 'max non-adjacent elements' DPs: one on slices[0..3n-2] and one on slices[1..3n-1], taking the best.
The inner DP is dp[i][j] = best sum using exactly j elements from the first i, where dp[i][j] = max(dp[i-1][j], dp[i-2][j-1] + slices[i-1]).

edge_inputs.description: greedy picking largest first fails; DP needed
circular adjacency: first and last are neighbours

stress.description: slices of length 498 (3*166) with random values 1-1000
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = size if size % 3 == 0 else size - (size % 3)
    n = max(3, n)
    return [[rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(n)]]
